reviews.review_msg: "i will leave this to your capable hands"
Decent professor, midterms were somewhat fair and final was not the worst. Would recommend doing the seminar questions and the webassign as those will make sure you know what you are doing

I thought Dr. K was a great professor. Her exams are incredibly fair, and as long as you attend the lectures, (ALL OF THEM, especially at the end of the semester), you'll do good. She highlights types of questions you will see on exams, and some of the hw she gives- specifically the seminar hw, will be v similar to the quizzes.  Good luck!!
I didn't like her initially, but once I started putting in some effort and just did my work I began to like her, and she was pretty helpful in tutorials. Reccomend taking her

Dr. Kouzniak has good lectures and her notes are very thorough, despite not finishing them on occasion. Offers lots of resources and office hours to succeed. However, the Math150 seminars were another story, as you'd get berated for not knowing how to do problems. Very hostile learning environment in the seminars, make sure u prepare well.

Midterms and finals were difficult. Quizzes consisting of 2 questions every week affect the average by 10 percent. If you wanna study 15 hours per week just for math then take it, otherwise pass this prof.

she is a great lecturer when she does finish notes, but most of the times she didnt. i liked her bc she was organized and offered video lecs w notes. however, seminars were an absolute nightmare. expect to get yelled at least once everytime ur in there. id only recommend math150 if u have no calc exp. if you do, take 151 instead and save ur sanity

I feel Dr. Kouzniak gets a lot of hate that she does not deserve. Although I did not get the grade I wanted, I had to respect her effort to really encourage students to do well. What I really enjoyed about her was the amount of resources from videos to seminar answer sheets. She pushes people alot which for some may seem bad, but she is just caring

If you have never taken calculus before in university then this course with Dr. K might be very challenging. Each day she covered a brand new topic and mostly skimmed through the intro and did the easy examples, therefore when doing homework, such as webassign and crowdmark seminars, the difficulty of the questions was a lot harder.

She was pretty good at teaching but would often rush through the lecture and skip over hard details. You have to be very specific in your answers to receive full marks and she was never inviting to ask questions.

She never finished a lecture fully and always had trouble managing her time. Very harsh towards those who would ask questions whether it was mid-class or office hours. She was an okay prof when it came to teaching but everything had to be solved her way or lots of marks would be deducted.

Great lectures and explanations. This class is a LOT of work and fast paced!! You need to put in the effort to do well. She can be slightly intimidating but will always try to ensure you understand the content. Midterms and final were fair but not easy.

Math 151 and lectures are fine. Don't take Math 150 with her. She comes to seminars, yells when work is not her style. Instead of two seminar questions that are due, expects you to bring entire seminar draft. Yelled at boy for being 2 min late about his bad midterm. Big difference in seminars with her and in Burnaby where its just TA and calm.

This class overall is a very heavy course load, and Natalia gave a lot of resources and information to help students. She was very accessible and put a lot of effort in to helping students improve. Overall I would say she is a great prof, just beware that Math 151 itself is difficult.

gave lots of resources, responded to emails quickly, lectures were helpful, overall an amazing prof. bad reviews are mostly from course content and have nothing to do with natalia's actual teaching ability, she listens to student feedback and tests are fair. Great prof overall

Dr. Kouzniak is a solid professor. Very easy midterms and decent quizzes if you study the material as all the content are questions from the weekly assignments. Funny during lectures, but just make sure it isn't at your expense! Try to attend all lectures leading up to the finals as she may give hints for the hard questions on assignments.
Amazing professor! seriously cares about her students and provides constructive criticism. Can be a bit harsh but she just wants the best. She is also hilarious!
She was hard to understand during lecture and did not go into enough detail on calculus concepts. She expected everyone to know info from MATH154, and was rude if you asked "dumb" questions.
I can see Dr. Kouzniak wanting her students to succeed. She's definitely not scary, but she has a straight forward teaching style since math is a tough subject. I'd recommend taking her.

Dr. Kouzniak is extremely rude toward her students and gets annoyed when anyone used the chat to ask questions. During midterms and weekly quizzes, she doesn't mute herself and continues to talk loudly, distracting everyone. Midterms are fair, but her lectures are unclear and you basically teach yourself the entire course. Avoid her if you can.

not mandatory expect the weekly friday quizzes which are so brutal. her lectures cover way too much, basically goes thru 3/4 of a chapter in one class. a couple times it got so bad during lecture that I literally just gave up and cried. my friend even dropped the class after first week. over 60 students have dropped this course since it started. GL

very rude during class, calls out peoples names for just asking questions. does not explain, skips over some examples and makes you go watch the videos. goes over the lecture notes way too fast, and does not like students asking questions; was about to close the chat for the whole semester.

Great prof! At first she seems quite scary and slightly rude, but once you get to know her you realize she is the complete opposite. She really cares about her students. She provides worksheets to do with the solutions. If you look over lecture notes, do the worksheets, do the tutorial problems, then nothing on the midterms will be a surprise.

She is extremely organized & efficient. Her midterms are tough , but fair. To do well in the class, you must put in the time and effort to rewrite lecture notes, thoroughly understand the weekly homework (solutions always posted after deadline), and do the provided extra problems. The final exam is a grade saver! Textbook only needed for hw q's.

I've had Natalia for 154 and 155. Her midterms are death and way too long for the 50 minutes she provides. Lots of homework and you need the textbook. Lecture are 100% mandatory if you want to pass. She is very motivated to teach her students and care greatly about our success, but comes off as rude. You'll learn that is just the way she teaches.
Exams are very tough, expects everyone to know everything, go to her classes and take notes! practise all the sample questions and tutorial questions or else you will suffer!
Midterms are nightmares; most questions you've never ever seen before. Overcomplicates concepts and theories.

She is willing to help and as teacher she has a really hardworking attitude. Mind you, her midterms are definitely a wake up call. I found the final to be not bad. She overcomplicates some of the theories and content sometimes. Good if you are good at calc because her scaling gave me a beautiful A but I definitely worked hard for that.

She may be intimidating to approach, but very helpful once you visit her office hours.. and trust me, you'll need it. VERY tough class, HARD exams, doesn't scale. Not recommended unless you have a solid understanding of calculus.

The only good thing I can really say about her is that she teaches concepts very well. She is very unapproachable. Her exams are extremely difficult. Majority of the questions require critical thinking, and its hard to solve those questions when you only have 50 minutes (midterms)

She is helpful, but trust me on this. DO NOT TAKE A CLASS WITH HER unless your a calculus genius. HER exams are extremely extremely hard and she doesn't even cut you any slack, no scaling or anything she needs to understand but she doesn't so no don't take a class with her.

Well how to start to even describe kouzniak she really teaches well and help you understand everything, she is quite scary to approach but is helpful. The one bad thing about her is her tests! midterms and finals are killers unless u completely understand its quite hard to get a decent mark.
She knows what she is talking about and is very helpful. Although she seems a bit scary, she really isnt and wants you to understand the material and do well.
Hard material

if you want to take calculus take it with another prof , her midterms and finals are not very related to the notes that she gives in the lecture or textbook,because she thinks that everyone is genius in math
SO HARD. Although she is very passionate about math and teaching she will not properly prepare you for the midterm/finals, they are way too hard.

She teaches the subject. But she'll never prepare you for the midterms or exams. Text question are useless. Compare her exams to any other teacher, she basically expects her students to be Calculus Genuises and then Doesnt scale. I self taught myself Math 154. I really struggled to get a decent grade in her class. Proceed with Caution!

So far my favourite prof at SFU. She is very willing to help and encourages questions. She doesn't tolerate tardiness or disruptions. For the most part, the midterms and final were extremely long and almost impossible to finish, but all questions were fair as long as you know the theory, not just the examples.

Natalia is an amazing professor! It is extremely important to attend her lectures (yes even if they are at 8:30 in the morning)- she doesn't like lates.  Study her notes, the textbook is good for examples not much else. Her exams are hard, but fair.

i took the course, but due to me despising math more than anything, i did horrible in her class, but even after failing a class and a semester of material that make me wanna throw up, she's awesome. so sweet, she explains everything very very well, just make sure you put in a REAL EFFORT!
excellent prof, the only reason I aced calculus!!

She's definitely a character. Her marking scheme is so bizzarre you don't even know how she comes up with your final mark, for good or worst. I had her twice and the first time I should have had a higher mark and the second time I got the mark I deserved. She's really just highly unpredictable, the final was killer, but midterms were easy.
Absolutely excellent prof! By far the best prof I've had in 3 yrs at SFU. Makes calculus understandable which is no small feat. Exams are fair and marked accordingly.

She is a pretty easy prof... easy marks and very approachable. *** Her tests are hard!!! and she teaches very fast, so try not to miss your class!! well be prepare when you see the "amount" of questions on your midterm.>;). she is a very nice prof and good at explain math ^^. VERY RECOMMENDED!

very helpful prof if you need her at anytime! tho very intimidating when helping you!her exams are very difficult. the final exam had many word problems which werent covered during lectures nor on hwk questions. midterms were fair. i enjoyed her class. she is very condsiderate!!

Natalia does a great job with her online notes. However, she speaks a very fast speed, (the worst part is she has a English accent). In addition, her exams are WAY too long and difficult. Most students find it extremely hard to complete the exams on time, and if they do finish, there is no or little time at all to double check the answers.

The professors teaching ability is very good. She clarifies things and teaches at ease. The course requires committment to do well. The professor is reduntanly repeats that attendance and being on time is very important.
She is AWESOME!!! Made me ...enjoy... calculus. Very, very, very helpful, very sweet. The best prof in math department!!! If you can take anything with her, go for it. You won't regret it.

Excellent notes. She has passion for this course when she is teaching it. If you ask a question and it seems that she is yelling at you... I dont think she means it. Oh... and make sure you come to class. She actually keeps tabs on people she sees and if she catches you in tutorial she will lecture you on coming to class.

Made calculus tolerable. Lovely notes- hand-written, but legible & posted well ahead of time on WebCT. Helpful all the time, friendly most of the time (but everyone has their bad moods sometimes), and usu. present in workshop. Encouraged class when we did well, which was a morale booster. Helped students on final, but ya didn't hear that from me ;)

in the 5 semesters that i've been at sfu shes the best prof that i've ever had!!! her notes are awesome and you can tell she just has a love for teaching and real will to see her students succeed. shes always willing to take extra time out of the schedule to help out students and just knows her stuff. the best at making complicated concept a breeze

yeah....she's good.  She can be intimidating sometimes wen students ask questions becuz her voiice goes too loud.  I think that's just how her voice is.  It has nothing to do wif her annoyance towards da student's question.  She certainly cares for her students and will do her best to hlep.

Best teacher ever. She is really in touch with her class and has an incredible ability to teach her students. She certainly enjoys teaching and that's very encouraging. Her classes are never boring because she always finds something interesting to do. She is an amazing teacher and if I had her for all my courses, then SFU would have been a breathe.
